policy_text: The Product Pricing Catalog establishes the authoritative commercial reference for NexWave Technologies' TeleSphere BSS/OSS Suite for RFP automation systems.
All prices in the catalog are in Indian Rupees (INR), exclusive of applicable GST (18% on software and professional services).

The catalog covers Implementation CapEx pricing by workstream, Annual Managed Services (AMS) OpEx by service tier, Software license structures, Optional and add-on modules, Volume-based pricing tiers for subscriber scale, and Payment milestone schedules.

The Five-Year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) structure is contractually anchored for Vi Business Solutions RFP-2026-VI-BSS-NXT-002 and serves as a reference baseline for Tier-1 telecom BSS/OSS transformation engagements.
source_section: Five-Year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Summary

policy_text: Year 0 Implementation CapEx is INR 312 Crores (one-time, milestone-linked).
source_section: Five-Year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Summary

policy_text: Year 1 OpEx (Post Go-Live Stabilization) is INR 68 Crores (AMS commencement from Phase 3 go-live).
source_section: Five-Year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Summary

policy_text: Year 2 OpEx is INR 72 Crores (4% escalation over Year 1).
source_section: Five-Year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Summary

policy_text: Year 3 OpEx is INR 76 Crores (4% escalation over Year 2).
source_section: Five-Year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Summary

policy_text: The document is classified as CONFIDENTIAL and applies only to authorized procurement personnel.
The document is effective as of February 2026 and applies to Vi Business Solutions RFP-2026-VI-BSS-NXT-002.
Year 4 OpEx is INR 79 Crores with a 4% escalation over Year 3.
Year 5 OpEx is INR 83 Crores with a 4% escalation over Year 4.
5-Year Total Cost of Ownership is INR 690 Crores, excluding optional modules and including cloud IaaS pass-through at cost.
Year 0 implementation cost is INR 312 Crores, structured across five delivery workstreams as defined in the RFP scope of work.
Stream A (Platform Design & Architecture) has an indicative cost of INR 22 Crores and a duration of 8 weeks (Phase 0).
Stream B (Software Deployment & Configuration) includes all BSS/OSS modules, 40+ integrations, and performance/security testing.
Stream C (Data Migration) involves 215M subscriber records, 24-month CDR history, and zero-revenue-leakage cutover.
Stream D (Training & Change Management) includes 200 super-users, 3,500+ ops staff, runbooks, and documentation.
Post-Go-Live Hypercare provides 90-day war-room support per phase with 24x7 dedicated team.
Post-Go-Live Hypercare duration is 90 days per phase.
Project Management & Governance includes a 150-person program team with PMO, architecture board, and QA governance.
Total Year 0 Implementation CapEx is INR 312 Crores over ~18 months.
Annual Managed Services (AMS) includes service tiers for Critical Systems (OCS, Charging, Fraud) with L2/L3 Incident Management and Proactive Monitoring.
Annual Managed Services (AMS) includes Billing & Revenue Assurance with L2/L3 Incident Management and Billing cycle support.
Post-Go-Live Hypercare is included in Year 0 CapEx budget.
Project Management & Governance team operates for full program duration.
Implementation phases are executed concurrently across all phases.
24x7x365 availability during bill run; business hours otherwise
source_section: 24x7x365 during bill run; business hours otherwise

policy_text: CRM & Order Management: L2 Incident + Configuration changes during business hours (8AM–8PM IST Mon–Sat)
Self-Care & Digital Channels: L2 Incident + Content updates during business hours Mon–Fri
Cloud Infrastructure (AWS): IaaS management, cost optimization, patching with 24x7 automated + business hours manual support
2 platform enhancement releases per year
Enhancement releases must follow the release plan
2 platform enhancement releases per year are included in the pricing
Major Enhancements (T&M) scope beyond 2 base releases requires separate quoting
Major Enhancements (T&M) are billed separately
The annual fee for Year 1 (FY2027–28) is INR 68 Cr.
The annual fee for Year 2 (FY2028–29) is INR 72 Cr.
The annual fee for Year 3 (FY2029–30) is INR 76 Cr.
The annual fee for Year 4 (FY2030–31) is INR 79 Cr.
The annual fee for Year 5 (FY2031–32) is INR 83 Cr.
The cumulative OpEx for Year 1 is INR 68 Cr.
The cumulative OpEx for Year 2 is INR 140 Cr.
The cumulative OpEx for Year 3 is INR 216 Cr.
The cumulative OpEx for Year 4 is INR 295 Cr.
The cumulative OpEx for Year 5 is INR 378 Cr.
The annual fee escalation is capped at 4% per year.
The annual fee escalation is the lower of 4% per year or the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) published by the Government of India.
The Extended Warranty (Years 6–7) has an annual maintenance cost of INR 41 Cr/year with no escalation.
The Extended Warranty (Years 6–7) must be exercisable 6 months before Year 5 expiry.
The legacy platform (Ericsson BSCS) has limited 4G capabilities and zero 5G roadmap.
The platform cannot support convergent billing for prepaid and postpaid in a single engine.
Order fulfillment cycles average 96 hours, resulting in significant churn at point-of-activation.
The client requires a complete platform replacement with zero tolerance for billing disruption during migration.
The migration duration is 22 months for the full program.
The solution supports convergent billing for prepaid and postpaid in a single engine.
The solution achieves zero billing disruption during migration, certified by the client CIO.
The Go-Live Date is December 2022 for the AsiaNet Wireless project.
The contract value is comparable to Tier-1 scale engagements.
The document is marked as Confidential, indicating legal obligations for information protection.
source_section: NexWave Technologies | Case Study Compendium | NWTECH-CS-2026-001 | Confidential

policy_text: The case studies serve as the knowledge base for RFP automation matching against subscriber scale, scope, technology stack, and regulatory context requirements.
The engagement type is Greenfield BSS/OSS Transformation.
The solution supports 120 million subscribers migrated from the legacy platform.
TeleSphere OCS v7.1 supports 3GPP Rel-16 Ro/Gy/Rf convergent charging for 120M subscribers.
TeleSphere Billing Engine generates convergent bills for postpaid and prepaid unified billing.
TeleSphere CRM provides a 360-degree customer view and replaces legacy Amdocs CRM.
TeleSphere OMS uses BPMN-based order orchestration with 45 pre-built order workflow types.
TeleSphere NIM eliminates shadow inventory across 180,000 sites.
TeleSphere DataBridge includes pre-built Ericsson BSCS certified extraction and transformation connectors.
Cloud deployment is active-active in AWS ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) and ap-southeast-3 (Jakarta).
NexWave deployed the TeleSphere Migration Factory framework: Extract → Cleanse → Transform → Load → Validate → Reconcile → Cutover.
Subscriber migration occurred in two phases: 12M enterprise/postpaid subscribers in Phase A, 108M prepaid in Phase B.
A mandatory 60-day parallel run was executed for both migration phases.
Legacy and new systems ran simultaneously with real-time reconciliation dashboards monitored 24x7 by dedicated teams in Manila and Bengaluru.
Zero billing disruption achieved: 100% subscriber records migrated with no billing errors.
OCS rating latency must be <41ms P99 at 1.5M TPS post go-live.
Order fulfillment cycles reduced from 96 hours to <3.8 hours average (96% improvement).
No-touch order rate increased to 94.7% (up from 28% on legacy OMS).
100% shadow inventory eliminated by decommissioning 6.2M Excel-based records.
Programme duration: 22 months (Phase A: 12 months; Phase B: 10 months).
Client sign-off by Maria Santos, Head of IT, AsiaNet Wireless.

This engagement is the closest direct reference to Vi's requirements: greenfield BSS/OSS, migration from Ericsson BSCS, 100M+ subscriber scale, zero-billing-disruption constraint, and convergent charging requirements.
Stellar Mobile case study involves a full BSS transformation with a prepaid focus.
Stellar Mobile has 280 million subscribers with the largest NexWave deployment.
Legacy platforms (Huawei SDP for prepaid and Amdocs Ensemble for postpaid) prevent convergent charging and cause revenue leakage estimated at 2.3% of annual revenue (~$340M).
Legacy platforms cannot scale beyond 800K TPS for real-time charging, causing peak-hour rate limiting and subscriber experience degradation.
The client's 5G rollout is blocked pending OCS readiness for 5G SA Nchf interface.
TeleSphere OCS v7.2 supports 3GPP Rel-17 Ro/Gy/Rf and 5G SA Nchf interfaces for unified convergent charging.
TeleSphere Billing Engine supports convergent bill generation for 280 million subscribers.
TeleSphere Revenue Assurance uses Apache Flink for real-time stream processing with sub-30-minute leakage detection.
TeleSphere Fraud Management employs LSTM-based ML models with SIM clone alert-to-block in <3 minutes.
Cloud infrastructure is deployed on AWS ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) with active-active configuration and auto-scaling for Ramadan/Lebaran peak traffic (3x normal TPS).
OCS rating latency P99 must be <38ms at 1.5M TPS, exceeding Vi's requirement of <50ms.
OCS must sustain 1.5M TPS in production with 2M TPS load-tested.
OCS availability must achieve 99.9997% over 12 months, exceeding five-nines (99.999%) target.
Revenue leakage must be reduced from 2.3% to <0.1% within 90 days of go-live.
OCS go-live date is October 2023.
OCS TPS production SLA is contractually guaranteed at 1.5M TPS.
SIM clone alert-to-block must occur within 3 minutes.
False positive rate for SIM clone detection must not exceed 0.4%.
5G SA Nchf interface is live and operational for network slice usage-based charging.
NexWave's production results exceed Vi OCS SLA thresholds (<50ms P99 at 1.5M TPS, 99.999% availability).
Full end-to-end 5G slice lifecycle management (Design → Order → Instantiate → Assure → Modify → Terminate) is implemented.
5G slice management complies with GSMA NG.116 and 3GPP TS 28.530 standards.
5G slice types deployed include eMBB (consumer 5G broadband), URLLC (enterprise low-latency), and mMTC (IoT/LPWA).
Enterprise self-service slice ordering via TeleSphere Enterprise Portal API is available.
Real-time KPI monitoring per slice with SLA violation alerts to enterprise customer portal within 2 minutes.
5G slice billing uses usage-based charging via 5G SA Nchf interface with per-slice CDRs and convergent invoices.
5G SA slicing go-live was completed by Q1 2024, 12 months from contract award.
NexWave has all three 5G slice types (eMBB, URLLC, mMTC) in commercial production.
NexWave has 4 enterprise customers on paid 5G slice contracts at go-live.
End-to-end slice provisioning and activation for eMBB slices must be completed in less than 8 minutes.
Real-time KPI alerts must be delivered to enterprise portals with SLA violations notified within 2 minutes.
Mohammed Al-Rashidi, VP IT, is available for Vi team site visits.
NexWave's Crescent Telecom deployment is a direct reference for Vi's 5G SA slice management requirements.
NexWave is one of very few vendors globally with live 5G SA slicing in production.
AsiaNet (120M) is a primary reference for Greenfield BSS/OSS deployments.
Stellar (280M) supports 100M+ subscriber migration with zero billing disruption.
Crescent (55M) partially supports 100M+ subscriber migration.
AsiaNet (120M) achieves OCS <41ms P99 at 1.5M TPS.
Stellar (280M) achieves OCS <38ms P99 at 1.5M TPS.
The system must maintain 99.999% availability for OCS.
The system must achieve 99.9997% availability for OCS.
Live 5G SA slice management is not in scope for this project.
Partial implementation of 5G SA slice management is limited to Nchf only.
Full 5G SA slice management is the primary reference implementation.
Migration from Ericsson legacy platform to BSCS is required.
Partial migration from Ericsson legacy platform to Huawei SDP is allowed.
Full migration from Ericsson legacy platform to Ericsson OSS is required.
Real-time Revenue Assurance must be implemented.
Revenue leakage detection must occur within <30 minutes.
The system must comply with TM Forum Frameworx Level 4 standards.

NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. must maintain a valid Goods and Services Tax Identification Number (GSTIN) of 29AAACN1234A1Z5 (Karnataka) as a legal requirement for tax compliance in India.
NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. possesses 15 years of technical and business capability in Telecom BSS/OSS operations since 2009, with 100% focus on the telecom sector.

source_section: 2.2 Key Financial Indicators for RFP Qualification

policy_text: The company must demonstrate net worth sufficient to support a ~INR 312 Crore CapEx engagement as a listed entity with a strong balance sheet.

policy_text: The company must confirm no insolvency proceedings, winding-up petitions, or disqualification orders via board resolution (Appendix A).

policy_text: The company must maintain audited financial statements for FY2023, FY2024, and FY2025 conducted by a Big 4 audit firm.
The company must maintain a telecom revenue percentage of total revenue at 12.8% (FY2023), 13.6% (FY2024), and 14.2% (FY2025).
The company must maintain a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.22 (FY2023), 0.20 (FY2024), and 0.18 (FY2025) to demonstrate low leverage.
The company must achieve YoY revenue growth of +14.5% (FY2024) and +12.3% (FY2025).
The company must meet the minimum INR 500 Crore telecom revenue threshold for 3 consecutive financial years as required by Vi RFP qualification criteria.
The company must maintain a net profit margin of 12.8% (FY2023), 13.6% (FY2024), and 14.2% (FY2025).

NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. serves 28 mobile operators across 18 countries with the TeleSphere Suite.
The tagline 'Powering India's Telecom Future' must be used on all India-market materials including proposal covers, executive summaries, and marketing decks.
NexWave communications must consistently project the brand personality traits: Expert, Reliable, Innovative, Committed.
The full legal name 'NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd.' must be used on first mention in all documents.
The product name 'TeleSphere BSS/OSS Suite' must be used in full form in all documents.
This document is designated for internal use only and must not be shared externally.
The Brand Guide governs all externally-facing proposal documents, RFP responses, marketing collateral, and client communications produced by NexWave Technologies' India operations.
This document serves as a knowledge base artifact for the RFP automation system to ensure generated proposals comply with brand standards.
The product name 'TeleSphere' must never be abbreviated as 'NWT' or 'NWTECH' in client documents; 'NWTECH' is restricted to internal use only.
The registered office address for proposals must be listed as '12th Floor, Embassy TechSquare, Outer Ring Road, Bengaluru – 560103' in all proposal documents.
The CIN 'U72200KA2009PTC123456' must only be used in legal documents and not in any other context.
The official website URL for all references must be 'www.nexwavetech.in' and no alternative URLs are permitted.
The color 'NexWave Red' (#E60000 / R:230 G:0 B:0) must be used exclusively for primary brand elements, headings, and call-to-action (CTA) buttons.
The color 'NexWave Navy' (#1A1A2E / R:26 G:26 B:46) must be used for body text and dark background applications.
The color 'Pure White' (#FFFFFF / R:255 G:255 B:255) must be used for page backgrounds and clean space design elements.
The color 'Light Gray' (#F5F5F5 / R:245 G:245 B:245) must be used for table alternating rows and callout sections.
The color 'Mid Gray' (#888888 / R:136 G:136 B:136) must be used for footer text, captions, and metadata.
Compliant Green (#1A7F3C) is designated as COMPLIANT for compliance status.
Partial Amber (#B8860B) is designated as PARTIAL for compliance status.
Alert Red (#CC0000) is designated as NON-COMPLIANT for critical alerts.
Light Red Tint (#FFF0F0) is used to highlight rows and totals.
Navy Light (#EEF2F7) is used for KV table labels and info panels.
Never use more than 3 colors on a single page.
Red is reserved for NexWave branding and may not be used for warnings unless combined with text.
Maintain WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios for all text: minimum 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for large text.
Document Title (cover) must use Arial font, 52pt / Bold, NexWave Navy for the main title of the proposal.
Sub-Title (cover) must use Arial font, 26pt / Regular, NexWave Red for the descriptive subtitle.
Heading 1 must use Arial font, 28pt / Bold, NexWave Navy with a red bottom border.
Primary font for client-facing documents must be Arial.
Do NOT use Calibri, Times New Roman, or decorative fonts in client-facing documents.
Fallback font for client-facing documents is Helvetica Neue.
Page size for India market documents must be A4 (210mm x 297mm), not US Letter.
All proposal documents must have 1 inch (72pt / 1440 DXA) margins on all sides.
Content width for A4 pages with 1-inch margins must be ~9,026 DXA / 6.27 inches.
Body text must use Arial 20pt Regular with black (#000000) color.
Headings must use Arial 24pt Bold with NexWave Red color.
Subheadings must use Arial 22pt Bold with NexWave Navy color.
Table headers must use Arial 18pt Bold with white text on navy (#1A1A2E) background.
Table body text must use Arial 18pt Regular with black text on white/light gray alternating rows.
Bullet points must use Arial 19pt Regular with black text.
Footers/metadata must use Arial 16pt Regular with mid gray (#888888) color.
Technical/code elements must use Courier New 18pt Regular with black text.
Body text must use single line spacing (1.0) with 8pt spacing before and after paragraphs.
Body text must be left-aligned; cover titles must be center-aligned.
Table cell padding must be Top: 80–100 DXA, Bottom: 80–100 DXA, Left: 120 DXA, Right: 120 DXA.
Headers on every page must include 'Company name | Document title | Classification level'.
Footers on every page must include 'Document reference | Version | Classification | Page number'.
Client proposals must include a cover page with the document title in 52pt Bold, NexWave Navy.
Client proposals must include a sub-title in 26pt NexWave Red with a red bottom border line.
Client proposals must include 'Prepared by / Submitted to' lines in 20pt Mid Gray.
Client proposals must include document reference, version, date, and classification in 18pt Mid Gray.
Client proposals must include a classification banner (CONFIDENTIAL / INTERNAL USE ONLY) in 18pt Bold, NexWave Red.
Table headers must have a NexWave Navy background (#1A1A2E), white text, and 18pt Bold formatting.
Table rows must alternate between white (#FFFFFF) and light gray (#F5F5F5) shading using ShadingType.CLEAR.
All table borders must be single line, 1pt, light gray (#DDDDDD).
Highlight/totals rows must use light red tint (#FFF0F0) with bold text.
Tables must specify both columnWidths (array) and individual cell widths (DXA) for consistent rendering.
Percentage widths must not be used in tables; only DXA values are allowed for cross-platform compatibility.
All RFP responses must include an Executive Summary section with 2 pages max, Vi-specific content, and quantified outcomes.
source_section: 5.1 Mandatory Sections for All RFP Responses

policy_text: Include NexWave GSTIN (29AAACN1234A1Z5), PAN (AAACN1234A), and TDS applicability note in the commercial section of India proposals.
Explicitly address TRAI, DOT, DPDP Act 2023, and GST e-invoicing compliance in the compliance matrix for India proposals.
State '100% India data residency' with MeitY empanelment reference in India proposals.
Proposals for India enterprise/telecom sector must be in English only; rural/government sector bids may be bilingual (Hindi + English).
All prices in India proposals must be in Indian Rupees (INR) with a forex protection statement ('all prices in INR; no forex exposure for client').
Payment terms for India proposals must be Net 30 days from invoice, with a milestone-linked structure and bank guarantee for advance payment.
Compliance matrix must include point-by-point responses to all mandatory and preferred RFP requirements.
Client references must include a minimum of 3 references with contact details and note availability for site visits.
Appendices must include certifications, CVs, financials, legal declarations, NDA, and insurance certificates.
Technical solutions must include a compliance matrix aligned with RFP section requirements.
Implementation methodology must include a phase plan, named team, milestone commitments, and critical blackout periods.
Data migration strategy must use the TeleSphere Migration Factory with a zero-leakage commitment and parallel run plan.
Support and AMS model must include an SLA schedule, hypercare plan, and 5-year AMS structure.
Commercial proposals must reference NWTECH-PPC-2026-001 and include milestone payments and TCO summary.
Company profile must reference NWTECH-CP-2026-001 for financials, certifications, and references.

NexWave Technologies holds ISO 9001:2015 certification issued by Bureau Veritas, last audited in January 2025, with validity under annual surveillance.
NexWave Technologies holds PCI-DSS Level 1 certification (version 4.0) issued by QSA KPMG India, last audited in December 2023, valid until December 2026 with renewal initiated.

source_section: 2. RFP Security Requirements — Full Compliance Matrix

policy_text: TLS 1.3 encryption in transit must be enforced for all inter-service and client-facing APIs.
source_section: 2. RFP Security Requirements — Full Compliance Matrix

policy_text: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) must be enforced for all admin portals.
source_section: 2. RFP Security Requirements — Full Compliance Matrix

policy_text: AWS Key Management Service (KMS) with CloudHSM must be used for key management.
source_section: 2. RFP Security Requirements — Full Compliance Matrix

policy_text: TLS 1.3 must be the enforced minimum version for encryption in transit.
source_section: 2. RFP Security Requirements — Full Compliance Matrix

policy_text: MFA must be implemented via Azure AD or Okta with SAML 2.0 / OAuth 2.0 / OIDC federation.
source_section: 2. RFP Security Requirements — Full Compliance Matrix

policy_text: NexWave Technologies implements Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) integrated with CyberArk Privileged Access Management (PAM) and Open Policy Agent (OPA) policy engine.
NexWave Technologies has deployed a GSMA FS.11/FS.19 certified signaling firewall (SS7/Diameter/SIP) at all 5 Tier-1 reference sites.
NexWave Technologies maintains immutable audit logs with 36-month retention using AWS CloudTrail, Splunk SIEM, and S3 Object Lock (WORM) technology.
NexWave Technologies' Bengaluru delivery center is ISO 27001:2022 certified by Bureau Veritas, with the most recent audit completed in February 2025.
The company holds a PCI-DSS v4.0 Level 1 certification with QSA KPMG India, valid until December 2026.
The company maintains a valid PCI-DSS certificate (Appendix B) and Attestation of Compliance (AOC).
Bi-annual external penetration testing is conducted by Mandiant and KPMG for PCI-DSS compliance.
Critical findings from penetration tests must be remediated within 30 days.
Penetration test engagement letters and a remediation tracker are maintained.
The company is MeitY-empanelled for AWS India regions ap-south-1 (Mumbai) and ap-south-2 (Hyderabad).
All data is stored in AWS ap-south-1/ap-south-2 regions with 100% India data residency.
Cross-border data transfers are prohibited.
Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) policies are enforced for data residency compliance.
The company complies with the DPDP Act 2023, including deployment of PII tokenization and consent management modules.
A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) template is prepared for DPDP Act 2023 compliance.
Disaster Recovery (DR) operations must achieve RPO <15 minutes and RTO <1 hour.
An active-active multi-region DR setup is implemented in Mumbai and Hyderabad.
Geo-failover is enabled via Route 53 for disaster recovery.
Disaster recovery tests are conducted every 6 months.
NexWave acknowledges and accepts obligations as a 'Data Processor' under the DPDP Act 2023 for all subscriber personal data processed on behalf of Vi.
NexWave provides a pre-drafted Data Processing Agreement (DPA) aligned to DPDP Act Sections 8 and 9, ready for execution at contract signing.
All MSISDN, Aadhaar-linked identifiers, and payment data are tokenized at ingestion using AWS Tokenization Service; raw PII is never stored in application databases.
TeleSphere Consent Module maintains granular per-subscriber consent records for marketing, data sharing, and profiling with a 3-year audit trail.
Zero subscriber data leaves India territory, confirmed by AWS data residency attestation and network egress monitoring.
Automated ILM policies enforce TRAI-mandated CDR retention (2 years active; 5 years archive) and subscriber deletion workflows compliant with DPDP erasure rights.
NexWave commits to notifying Vi within 72 hours of a confirmed personal data breach under DPDP Act Section 8(6).
Automated TRAI QoS reports are generated via TeleSphere Regulatory Reports module with no manual intervention.
TeleSphere supports DOT-mandated Lawful Intercept and Monitoring (LIM) requirements with CALEA-compatible architecture.
TAP/NRTRDE interfaces are implemented for international roaming compliance, with BARG/TADIG compliance.
Automated reconciliation ensures TRAI revenue figures match internal billing records within 0.01% tolerance.
NexWave is a PCI-DSS Level 1 Service Provider, the highest tier for entities processing >6 million card transactions/year.
Cardholder data environment (CDE) is isolated from non-payment systems via network segmentation and dedicated key management.
Payment gateway integrations (NPCI/UPI, Razorpay, Paytm) are pre-certified; no new cardholder data flows are introduced without PCI change control.
Annual on-site PCI-DSS audits by QSA (KPMG India), quarterly internal vulnerability scans, and bi-annual external penetration testing are conducted.
NexWave deploys Vi's BSS/OSS platform on AWS India (ap-south-1 Mumbai primary; ap-south-2 Hyderabad DR).
AWS India is MeitY-empanelled (MeitY-CSP-AWS-2024) for government and regulated sector workloads including telecom.
AWS India regions are ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certified, with annual SOC 2 Type II reports available under NDA.
All data must be stored in ap-south-1 (Mumbai) and ap-south-2 (Hyderabad) with no cross-border transfers allowed.
AWS KMS and CloudHSM with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM are used for key management, with customer-managed keys (CMK) available for Vi.
NexWave SOC (Bengaluru) must respond to P1 security events within 15 minutes and achieve initial containment within 1 hour.
Critical (CVSS 9.0+) penetration test findings must be remediated within 30 days of identification.
High-severity penetration test findings must be remediated within 60 days of identification.
Personal data breaches must be notified to Vi within 72 hours and to the Data Protection Board of India as required by the DPDP Act.
NexWave operates a responsible disclosure program and tracks CVEs for all TeleSphere components.
Recovery Point Objective (RPO) must be less than 15 minutes using AWS active-active replication with <5-second lag for transaction logs.
Recovery Time Objective (RTO) must be less than 1 hour via automated geo-failover using Route 53 health checks with no manual intervention.
Disaster recovery (DR) failover tests must be conducted every 6 months, with test reports provided to Vi's IT governance team.
A 24x7 Hypercare War Room must be maintained for 90 days post each phase go-live, co-located at Vi's Mumbai HQ and Bengaluru delivery center.
Professional Indemnity insurance coverage of INR 100 Crores per claim must be maintained with New India Assurance Co. for FY2026-27, renewable annually.
The Standard Terms & Conditions govern the relationship between NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. and the Client for BSS/OSS platform modernization services.
The Terms are incorporated by reference into the Master Services Agreement (MSA) and all associated Statements of Work (SOW).
In case of conflict between the Terms and an executed MSA, the MSA prevails.
The Master Services Agreement includes these Terms, all SOWs, SLA schedules, pricing exhibits, and data processing agreements.
Deliverables include software modules, configurations, documents, reports, data migration artifacts, or other outputs produced by the Vendor as specified in the applicable SOW.
Services include implementation, software deployment, data migration, training, hypercare support, and Application Managed Services (AMS) as defined in the relevant SOW.

The TeleSphere Suite is NexWave's proprietary BSS/OSS software platform, including modules like OCS, Billing Engine, CRM, OMS, NIM, Revenue Assurance, Fraud Management, Self-Care, and SliceOrchestrator.
Subscriber Data includes personal and non-personal data relating to the Client's subscribers, such as MSISDN records, CDRs, billing history, consent records, and CRM data.
A phase of the platform is accepted as production-ready only after successful completion of acceptance testing criteria defined in the SOW.
The document applies to all MSA engagements for BSS/OSS platform delivery and Application Managed Services.
The AMS Commencement Date is the date from which the Application Managed Services contract commences billing, as specified in the commercial exhibit — typically the Phase 3 Go-Live Date.
Any modification to the agreed SOW scope, timeline, or commercial terms must be formalized through the Change Control Process defined in Clause 7.
The Vendor shall mobilize the programme team as specified in the SOW, including the named key personnel (Programme Director, Architecture Lead, and Domain Leads), from the contract effective date.
Named key personnel shall not be replaced without the Client's prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.
In the event of an unavoidable replacement of named key personnel, the Vendor shall provide a candidate with equivalent or superior qualifications for Client approval within 15 business days.
The Vendor commits to the milestone schedule defined in the applicable SOW.
The Vendor shall notify the Client in writing at least 20 business days in advance if it becomes aware that a milestone will not be met.
Unexcused delays beyond 30 days on critical path milestones shall trigger the delay credits defined in Schedule 2 (SLA & Service Credits).
For all subscriber data migration activities, the Vendor guarantees zero billing disruption and zero revenue leakage as defined in the Migration Acceptance Criteria (Appendix to SOW).
Acceptance of data migration requires: (i) 100% subscriber record count reconciliation, (ii) CDR value reconciliation within 0.01% tolerance, and (iii) parallel run sign-off by Client's billing team.
Any billing errors attributable to Vendor migration activities discovered within 90 days of Go-Live shall be remedied by the Vendor at no additional cost.
The platform shall meet or exceed the performance benchmarks specified in Schedule 1 (SLA Schedule) from the Go-Live Date.

Performance benchmarks include OCS rating latency (<50ms P99 at 1.5M TPS), availability (99.999% for OCS/Charging), bill run completion (10M accounts in <6 hours), and all other metrics in Schedule 1.
Failure to meet performance benchmarks triggers service credits per Schedule 2.
Implementation payments are milestone-linked as specified in the Commercial Exhibit.
AMS payments are monthly in arrears from the AMS Commencement Date.
All amounts are exclusive of applicable GST (currently 18% on software and professional services).
Invoices shall be raised by the Vendor within 5 business days of milestone achievement, accompanied by evidence of completion.
Client shall pay undisputed invoices within Net 30 days of receipt.
For disputed invoices, the Client shall raise a written dispute within 15 business days with specific grounds.
Both parties shall resolve disputes within 30 days of the dispute notice.
Undisputed portions of disputed invoices shall be paid within the original Net 30 timeline.
Overdue amounts beyond 45 days shall accrue interest at SBI MCLR (1-year) + 2% per annum, calculated daily.
Persistent non-payment beyond 90 days (with a 30-day cure notice) shall entitle the Vendor to suspend non-critical services upon written notice.
AMS fees shall escalate annually at the lower of: (a) 4% per year (contractually committed), or (b) the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) published by the Ministry of Commerce, Government of India.
The AMS price escalation cap is a contractual commitment and cannot be exceeded.
AMS price escalation applies from the second AMS year onward.
All changes to agreed SOW scope, timeline, or commercial terms shall be governed by the Change Control Process.
Either party may raise a Change Request ('CR') in writing using the standard CR form.
The Vendor shall provide a commercial and timeline impact assessment within 10 business days of a Change Request.
The Client shall approve, reject, or counter-propose a Change Request within 10 business days of receiving the impact assessment.
No change shall be implemented without a written Change Order signed by both parties' authorized representatives.
Change Requests that reduce scope shall not entitle the Vendor to additional payment, but may reduce milestone-linked payments proportionally as agreed.
Service levels defined in the SLA schedule are material terms of the Agreement.
The SLA schedule defines availability, latency, response, and resolution targets for each system component.
SLA measurement periods, exclusions (scheduled maintenance, force majeure, Client-caused outages), and credit calculation methodologies are defined in Schedule 1.
The OCS/Charging Engine has an SLA target of 99.999% availability in a calendar month.
A credit trigger for the OCS/Charging Engine is <99.999% availability in a calendar month.
The monthly credit rate for the OCS/Charging Engine is 2% of the monthly AMS fee per 0.001% shortfall in availability.

The Vendor's total aggregate liability under the Agreement shall not exceed the greater of: (a) the total fees paid by the Client in the 12 months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim, or (b) INR 100 Crores.

policy_text: Either party may terminate the Agreement if the other party commits a material breach and fails to remedy it within 60 days of written notice.
Either party may terminate the Agreement if the other party becomes insolvent, enters liquidation, or is subject to a winding-up order.
Either party may terminate the Agreement if the other party engages in corrupt practices or violates applicable anti-bribery laws.
If the Client terminates the Agreement for Vendor's cause, the Vendor shall refund all milestone payments for undelivered milestones within 90 days.
The Client may terminate the AMS contract for convenience with 6 months' written notice after Year 2 of the AMS term.
Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of all completed milestone payments.
Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of reasonable mobilization costs for work-in-progress (not to exceed 20% of remaining implementation CapEx).
Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of committed third-party costs that cannot be cancelled.
The Vendor shall provide exit assistance for up to 12 months after termination or expiry of the Agreement.
Exit assistance includes knowledge transfer to the Client's designated successor vendor, data extraction in standard formats (CSV/JSON), platform documentation, and API specifications.
Exit assistance is charged at the T&M rates defined in the Commercial Exhibit.
The Vendor's obligation to maintain the platform continues during the exit assistance period.
Disputes must first be resolved through designated project representatives within 15 business days of a dispute notice.
Unresolved disputes must be escalated to senior management (CXO level) for a 30-day resolution attempt.
Unresolved disputes must be submitted to a mutually agreed mediator (ICADR or DIAC) for structured mediation within 45 days.
Unresolved disputes must be submitted to binding arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 (as amended).
The seat of arbitration must be Mumbai, Maharashtra.
The language of arbitration must be English.
The number of arbitrators must be 1 (sole arbitrator by mutual agreement) or 3 (if parties cannot agree within 30 days).
The arbitration award must be final and binding.
The Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India.
Jurisdiction for interim relief must be the Courts of competent jurisdiction at Mumbai, Maharashtra.
All services must be containerized using Docker/Helm and deployed on Kubernetes (EKS) with infrastructure-as-code via Terraform.
The platform must use an event-driven microservices architecture with Apache Kafka v3.6 as the central event bus, supporting 800K+ events/second.
All services must expose TM Forum Open API-compliant REST and AsyncAPI natively.
The platform must maintain an active-active multi-region deployment with Mumbai as primary and Hyderabad as DR site, including automated geo-failover and no single point of failure.
Horizontal scalability must be achieved via Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA), tested to support 300M subscribers without re-architecture.
Observability must be implemented using Prometheus + Grafana for metrics, ELK stack for log aggregation, Jaeger for distributed tracing, and Splunk SIEM for security.
TeleSphere OCS must comply with 3GPP Rel-17 Ro/Gy/Rf + Nchf standards.
TeleSphere OCS must support 280M subscribers and 1.5M TPS (as demonstrated in production at Stellar Mobile).
TeleSphere CRM must adhere to TMF632 standard for data modeling.

policy_text: The system supports 3GPP Release 17 Ro/Gy/Rf interfaces (Ro for online charging, Gy for policy & charging, Rf for offline CDR) natively.
The system supports 5G SA Nchf interface for network slice charging, which is live at Crescent Telecom (Q1 2024) and supports production usage-based charging.
OCS rating latency must achieve P99 <50ms at 1.5M TPS, with <38ms P99 demonstrated in production.
The system guarantees peak throughput of 1.5M TPS, with 2M TPS load-tested and 1.5M TPS production SLA contractually guaranteed.
The system scales to support up to 300M subscribers, with 280M live at Stellar Mobile and horizontal scaling tested to 300M without re-architecture.
The system supports prepaid real-time balance deduction, reservation, and reauthorization with <38ms P99 latency.
The company uses CDR-based mediation for convergent bill generation in postpaid and prepaid unified billing.
Enterprise contract billing must integrate with CPQ systems to support volume discounts and SLA-based credits for enterprise accounts.
OCS availability must meet 99.999% (Five-Nines) uptime as a contractual commitment.
Stellar Mobile achieved 99.9997% OCS availability over 12 months post-go-live, exceeding the contractual requirement.
The primary data store for OCS balance management is Apache Cassandra, ensuring high-throughput and sub-millisecond read/write P99 performance.
Session state management for real-time session reservation and balance locks uses Redis Cluster.
CDR mediation processes data through Apache Kafka to a custom mediation engine, with PostgreSQL as the billing input store.
OCS and Billing systems must use separate data stores to avoid billing back-pressure during peak charging periods.
TeleSphere CRM v5.1 supports unified customer management for consumer prepaid, postpaid, SME, and enterprise segments.
The CRM provides a 360-degree view of customer interactions across call center, web, app, and retail POS channels.
CRM screen load time must be <1.4 seconds P99 in production, meeting the RFP target of <2 seconds.
The CRM includes an AI agent assist feature with real-time sentiment analysis, suggested responses, and automated case routing.
ML-based churn prediction with 30-day propensity scores and next-best-action recommendations must be implemented.
TeleSphere CRM v5.1 uses a Salesforce-compatible data model.
TeleSphere CRM v5.1 includes a Siebel v8.x bidirectional sync adapter.
TeleSphere CRM v5.1 provides history migration tooling.
TeleSphere CRM v5.1 data model is aligned with TM Forum SID standards.
TeleSphere CRM v5.1 natively exposes the TMF632 Party Management API.
TeleSphere OMS v4.3 uses a microservices architecture.
TeleSphere OMS v4.3 employs a BPMN-based workflow engine (Camunda v7).
TeleSphere OMS v4.3 implements event-driven order orchestration.
TeleSphere OMS v4.3 includes 45+ pre-built workflows for SIM swap, plan change, number porting, enterprise provisioning, and 5G slice ordering.
TeleSphere OMS v4.3 achieves 94.7% no-touch order completion (AsiaNet Wireless Q1 2025).
TeleSphere OMS v4.3 requires no-touch automation to exceed 90%.
SIM swap/plan change orders must be fulfilled in <2 hours average (RFP target: <4 hours) with 50% headroom.
New connection orders must be fulfilled in <4 hours, including SIM provisioning and network activation.
Enterprise orders (complex CPQ + contract billing) must be fulfilled in <8 hours.
TeleSphere OMS v4.3 natively exposes the TMF622 Product Order Management API.
Real-time stream processing (Apache Flink)
Sub-30-minute leakage detection
OCS events, mediation records, billing invoices, network CDRs reconciled in real-time
Hybrid rule-based + ML model ensemble for fraud detection
LSTM-based anomaly detection for fraud detection
SIM Clone Alert-to-Block time <3 minutes in production
SIM Clone Alert-to-Block compliant with 40% headroom
Daily automated retraining of ML models
ML model false positive rate <0.5% (production-validated)
Pre-built migration toolkit for SUBEX RAG Migration
RA history preserved during SUBEX RAG Migration
No manual data re-entry during SUBEX RAG Migration
Automated QoS and revenue report generation for TRAI regulatory compliance
No manual intervention required for TRAI regulatory reports
Vendor support for Ericsson, Nokia, Huawei, ZTE with pre-built adapters
Discovery protocols (SNMP v2c/v3, NETCONF/YANG, TR-069, SSH, REST APIs) natively supported
Physical, logical, and service topology views with live operational status
Automated reconciliation to eliminate shadow inventory (decommissions Excel-based systems within 90 days)
TMF637 Product Inventory Management API natively exposed for real-time inventory queries
